abstract: The scene that briefly keeps a header at the top of the screen as the person scrolls through the article.
overview:
## Discussion

In the `FadingStickyHeader` scene, the header briefly “sticks” to the top of the screen as the person scrolls down through the article (away from the header) as shown in [https://developer.apple.com/apple-news/videos/fading-sticky-header.mp4].

You can use a `fading_sticky_header` with a [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Section] or [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Chapter] component that has a child `header` component. (The `section` or `chapter` component must contain a component with a `role` of `header`.) The header fades to the color you define in the `fadeColor` property of the `fading_sticky_header` scene.

### Example

items.description: A combination of animations and behaviors to use in sections and chapters that have headers.
items.name: Scene

items.description: The scene that gives the impression of a parallax effect by zooming out and scrolling slightly more slowly than the person’s action.
items.name: ParallaxScaleHeader

title: FadingStickyHeader
